summary:
An interdental papilla may protrude due to improper placement during application of the rubber dam; specifically when puncture sites are placed extremely proximal to these soft tissue areas allowing them easy access and potential egress into the space created by the tear or gap around the teeth. Agent2 reasoned based primarily on how proximity affects likelihood of penetration while performing procedures with rubber dams. Key point was misplacement leading ease-of-passage issues resulting directly related anatomical location concerns regarding specific surgical site preparation techniques involving barrier materials utilized intraorally.
